content.claim: Hungarian government has selectively invoked PISA equity findings to legitimize reforms but implementation has lagged; PISA reception revealed skepticism and has been mediated by local cultural contexts and institutional disorganization, preventing alignment with OECD-recommended approaches
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Analysis is qualitative and descriptive rather than quantifying degree of policy slippage
Does not specify which equity reforms were proposed, rejected, or delayed; attribution of delay to skepticism vs. resource constraints unclear
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
